name: Quarterly Ad Performance Review
description: Comprehensive quarterly review of paid performance for marketing-leadership audiences. Replaces 1–2 days of compilation per cycle with a defensible memo grounded in governed definitions.
alsoSurfaces: What did paid produce this quarter: spend, ROAS, pipeline, revenue?
Which channels grew, which declined, and by how much?
Where did efficiency improve or deteriorate?
What changed structurally: channel adds, pauses, creative refreshes, and how did it land?
beforeWeRun: Which quarter: last completed, or custom?
Audience tone: marketing leadership (detailed) or board-ready (concise)?

whatYoullGet.name: Executive summary
whatYoullGet.desc: Three to five sentences: total spend, blended ROAS, period-over-period direction, the single most important takeaway.

whatYoullGet.name: Quarter highlights
whatYoullGet.desc: 5–8 bullets: channels that grew or declined, efficiency changes, notable campaign wins or losses.

whatYoullGet.name: Channel performance
whatYoullGet.desc: Sortable table: channels as rows, columns for spend, attributed pipeline/revenue, ROAS, CAC, PoP deltas.

whatYoullGet.name: Efficiency trends
whatYoullGet.desc: Two paragraphs on efficiency trajectory and volume-vs-efficiency tradeoffs.

whatYoullGet.name: What changed
whatYoullGet.desc: 3–5 paragraphs on structural changes during the quarter and their observed impact.

whatYoullGet.name: Next quarter recommendations
whatYoullGet.desc: Channels to scale, channels to reduce, experiments to consider, each tied to specific data.

whatYoullGet.name: Sources & definitions
whatYoullGet.desc: Canonical attribution model, ROAS variant, period boundaries, sources read with row counts.

callout: 4 quick inputs, then Sage drafts your quarterly review in ~16 min.
